data.title: M-tron Industries, Inc. (NYSE: MPTI) secures $20M contract with US Department of Defense for RF components.
data.description: On December 11, 2025, in Orlando, Florida, M-tron Industries, Inc. (NYSE American: MPTI), a prominent supplier of high-performance radio frequency ("RF") components and solutions, revealed a significant deal worth around $20 million. The order was secured from a major prime contractor of the U.S Department of Defense.

data.title: NYSE offers daily pre-market update; TIME showcases 2025 Person of the Year at NYSE. Kristen Scholer delivers update.
data.description: On December 11, 2025, the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) issued its daily pre-market update live from the NYSE Trading Floor. Investors can gain valuable market insights before the trading day commences by checking out today's NYSE Pre-market update. Kristen Scholer presented the pre-market update on this day, offering crucial information to traders and investors.

data.title: Memory prices to rise sharply in 1Q26, causing cost pressure on manufacturers. Smartphone and notebook brands affected.
data.description: In recent studies conducted by TrendForce, it has been discovered that memory prices are expected to experience a substantial increase once more during the initial quarter of 2026. This surge will place substantial financial strain on worldwide end-device producers. As a result, smartphone and notebook companies will face challenges due to this price hike...

data.title: Give the gift of health with the jscreen jGift this holiday season, a simple way to share genetic testing - jscreen.org.
data.description: In Atlanta on December 11, 2025, jscreen launched the jGift for the holiday season. This innovative gift allows people to give the gift of health by providing access to genetic testing through jscreen.org, a national nonprofit organization. The jGift aims to make genetic testing accessible and meaningful, enabling individuals to share this health benefit with loved ones.

data.title: Berger Montague advises MoonLake Immunotherapeutics investors to contact the firm before Dec. 15, 2025 regarding a class action lawsuit against the company (MLTX) purchased during the period of March.
data.description: A class action lawsuit has been filed by leading law firm Berger Montague PC against MoonLake Immunotherapeutics (NASDAQ: MLTX) on behalf of investors who bought the Company's shares between March and December 2025. The suit alleges misconduct causing financial harm to shareholders. Details of the case include specific dates and actions taken by MoonLake.

data.title: MOOC market trends report highlights shift to interactive, AI-powered platforms for personalized learning.
data.description: A recent report on the massive open online course (MOOC) market highlights the changing technology landscape in this sector. The analysis, now available from ResearchAndMarkets.com, reveals a shift from conventional content delivery approaches to more interactive, AI-driven learning platforms. This evolution has resulted in enhanced personalization of learning experiences and a boost in course completion rates. The report outlines the trends and opportunities within the MOOC market, reflecting the significant developments in recent years.

data.title: NanOlogy announces program for rare pediatric brainstem tumor, targeting Diffuse Intrinsic Pontine Glioma. Clinical trial set for late 2026.
data.description: Cutting-edge intratumoral technology is being developed to specifically target Diffuse Intrinsic Pontine Glioma (DIPG). Investigations into the investigational new drug are currently ongoing, with plans to commence clinical trials in late 2026. Headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas, NanOlogy, LLC, a private oncology company, is leading the charge in this innovative approach. The significant development was announced on December 11, 2025.

data.title: After Trump's tariffs, US trade deficit decreased in September as imports and exports rose, hitting new lows.
data.description: After the implementation of the president's global tariffs, there was a slight increase in U.S. imports and exports compared to the previous month. Despite this, the trade deficit continued to decrease. The numbers show a gradual improvement in trade, with imports and exports seeing a rise from the recent lows. This indicates a positive trend in the trade sector, although the deficit is still declining.

data.title: Shentel completes $50 million project to expand broadband to 7,000 homes in Shenandoah County, VA with VATI program support.
data.description: Shenandoah Telecommunications Company (Shentel) has recently announced the successful conclusion of its Virginia Telecommunications Initiative (VATI) project in Edinburg, Va., on Dec. 11, 2025. This initiative was aimed at extending gigabit broadband internet access to over 7,000 households in Shenandoah County, Virginia, which were previously without service. The completion of this project, totaling a construction cost of $50 million, was made possible through a combination of funds from the VATI program under the Virginia Department of Housing and Community Development, Shentel’s internal investment, and a financial contribution from Shenandoah County. This effort significantly boosts broadband accessibility in the region.

data.title: Micro fulfillment market technology is evolving with companies like AutoStore, Opex, and Swisslog leading the way. Changes have been made towards shuttle-based systems and G2P robots, improving warehouse automation and fulfillment.
data.description: In Dublin, a recent report titled "Technology Landscape, Trends and Opportunities in Micro Fulfillment Market" has been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's portfolio. The micro fulfillment market has witnessed notable technological advancements in recent times, particularly in solutions offered by industry leaders such as AutoStore, shuttle-based systems, goods-to-person (G2P) robots, and automated guided vehicles (AGVs). These innovations have significantly enhanced warehouse automation, leading to optimized storage and retrieval systems, and improved fulfillment processes.

data.title: Standard Nuclear, Inc. in TN partners with the U.S. Department of Energy to deploy TRISO nuclear fuel line, aiming for 2026 reactor demos, under an Other Transaction Agreement (OTA).
data.description: In Oak Ridge, Tennessee, Standard Nuclear, Inc., a TRISO nuclear fuel manufacturer, has signed an Other Transaction Agreement (OTA) with the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE). This agreement signifies a significant step towards establishing privately funded advanced nuclear-fuel production infrastructure. The aim is to support TRISO-fueled reactor demonstrations scheduled for 2026. This development aligns with federal priorities outlined in recent executive actions.

data.title: Klarna teams up with Privy to create user-friendly crypto wallet; just launched KlarnaUSD with Tempo and Bridge.
data.description: Klarna, a global digital bank and payments provider, has partnered with Privy, a wallet infrastructure platform and a Stripe company, to research and develop wallet solutions for crypto products for Klarna users. This collaboration comes after Klarna's introduction of its stablecoin KlarnaUSD in partnership with Tempo and Bridge. The aim is to create a wallet to enhance the user experience, as Klarna ventures into the world of digital currencies.

data.title: Tryg's newsletter "Modelling investment results" details de-risking of portfolio, helping analysts model future results accurately.
data.description: In its latest newsletter titled "Modelling Investment Results," Tryg has shared insights on its ongoing de-risking strategy for its investment portfolio. The newsletter highlights the role of the match and free portfolios in enhancing the stability and predictability of investment income. By offering enhanced transparency, Tryg aims to enable analysts and capital market participants to more accurately predict future outcomes. This initiative underscores Tryg's commitment to prudent financial management, with focused efforts on achieving a more secure investment profile.

data.title: Metropolitan Dermatology offers same-day appointments at most of its 22 locations in New Jersey and Staten Island.
data.description: In Clark, New Jersey, on December 11, 2025, Metropolitan Dermatology, a prominent dermatology group operating 22 facilities throughout New Jersey and Staten Island, has introduced same-day appointments at the majority of its locations. This move aims to enhance accessibility to expert dermatologic services for patients.

data.title: K2 Space raised $250M in Series C funding at $3B valuation for new high-capability satellites.
data.description: A California company, K2 Space, revealed on December 11, 2025, that they have successfully secured a $250 million Series C funding round at a valuation of $3 billion. This investment will enable them to expedite the production of advanced spacecraft designed for the heavy-lift era.

data.title: Ilya Movshovich is revolutionizing sports with Digital Athlete Ventures; transforming Cobh Ramblers FC.
data.description: On December 11, 2025, in Miami, Ilya Movshovich, the Managing Partner at Digital Athlete Ventures and owner of Cobh Ramblers FC, highlighted the significance of innovation in professional sports beyond simply acquiring top-notch gear or costly players.movements_$_With the ever-increasing competitive nature of sports, Movshovich emphasized the need for unconventional approaches to drive success in the industry.

data.title: BAM Capital sells Oakdale Square Apartments, 200-unit community in Bloomington, delivering exceptional returns to investors.
data.description: In Indianapolis, on December 11, 2025, BAM Capital, a multifamily syndication firm headquartered in Indianapolis, IN, disclosed the prosperous sale of Oakdale Square Apartments, a 200-unit complex in Bloomington, Indiana. The deal signifies a profitable outcome for the company.

data.title: Health system and hospital leaders are investing in value-based care, with few having substantial revenue at risk. 101 leaders surveyed.
data.description: A recent survey conducted among 101 health system and hospital C-suite executives uncovered a trend towards heightened involvement in value-based care models aimed at enhancing care quality and reducing overall costs. However, the findings indicate that only a small number of these leaders have significant revenue exposure in these models. The survey outcomes were disclosed in a research report released in Baltimore on December 11, 2025.
